digest.component_or_area: LLM gate verdict plumbing (plan-review / code-review / completion-verify gates)
propositions: A systemic LLM failure across the three gates must block-but-retryable via a new exit code 11 (unsigned), while per-criterion fail-open is preserved so one flaky finder among N does not block a claim.

resolution_class and retryable are sourced from mamba's classify_llm_failure LLMOutcome and plumbed through the two verdict-returning gates: shape-A write-sites copy them onto coverage, and shape-B (_completion_precheck / _verify_completion) reads .outcome to set CommandError returncode 11 vs 1.
PydanticAIRunner.preflight must attach .outcome (via classify_llm_failure) to its raised LLMUnavailableError so pre-call outages carry disposition.

A never-sign guard at the sign-decision callers (review_plan, close_ticket) structurally refuses to call sign_manifest when the verdict is non-PASS or degraded, and a pre-sign head-SHA fingerprint recheck inside the write lock skips signing on code drift (close still exits 0 with a stderr warning), keeping verify outside the write lock.

The MCP tools review_plan/review_code/verify_completion in _mcp_llm.py return a structured failure dict {error, resolution_class, retryable, diagnostic} on a raised LLMError instead of propagating an opaque FastMCP tool error.

Optional coverage.resolution_class/retryable/diagnostic fields are added to plan_review_verdict and code_review_verdict schemas (completion_verdict unchanged), with exit-11 documented in docs/exit-codes.md, pinned by test_exit_codes.py, and two ADRs (exit-11, diagnostic-sanitization) authored.
